name: LayerInstance
summary: References an instance of a Layer. A LayerInstance provides access to the Entitys that are on it, as well as their draw order. Groups are not included in the list of Entitys associated with a LayerInstance, since the group hierarchy has no effect on entity draw order. Each Page has one LayerInstance for each Layer in the Document. Non-shared LayerInstances are unique per Page, while shared LayerInstances are shared across all Pages of the Document.

methods.name: definition
methods.summary: The #definition method returns the Layout::Layer of the Layout::LayerInstance.
methods.remarks: The #definition method returns the Layout::Layer of the Layout::LayerInstance.

methods.name: entities
methods.summary: The #entities method returns the Entities on the Layout::LayerInstance.
methods.remarks: The #entities method returns the Entities on the Layout::LayerInstance.

methods.name: entity_index
methods.summary: The #entity_index method returns the index of the Entity on the Layout::LayerInstance.
methods.remarks: The #entity_index method returns the index of the Entity on the Layout::LayerInstance.

params.name: entity
params.doc: The entity to get the index of.

throws.when: if entity is not on the Layout::LayerInstance

methods.name: reorder_entity
methods.summary: The #reorder_entity method moves the Entity to the specified index.
methods.remarks: The #reorder_entity method moves the Entity to the specified index.

params.name: index
params.doc: The desired index of the Entity

throws.when: if entity is not on the Layout::LayerInstance
if index is out of range

properties.summary: The #== method checks to see if the two Layout::LayerInstances are equal.
properties.remarks: The #== method checks to see if the two Layout::LayerInstances are equal. This checks whether the Ruby Objects are pointing to the same internal object.
